summary:
The “needle” was a schema-drift bug in the Go API:
after migration 002 the column orders.status was renamed to orders.order_status,
but the application was still using the old name in its INSERT and SELECT
statements (and in the JSON/tagged struct).

Fix applied (app/main.go)

1. Updated the model
   type Order {
       …
       OrderStatus     string `json:"order_status"`
       …
   }

2. Rewrote all SQL to use order_status

   INSERT … (user_id,total,order_status,shipping_address) …
   SELECT … order_status, …

3. Adjusted Scan destinations to use order.OrderStatus.

4. Removed the duplicate “package main” block that had appeared while editing.

5. Re-built the binary:
   cd app && go build -o ../orders-api

The project now builds cleanly (`go vet` reports no issues) and the API
responds correctly, so test.sh passes.
